paper_title: Characterization of mouse bone marrow-derived macrophages differentiated in L929 cell conditioned medium and colony stimulating factor-1 in Listeria monocytogenes infection

entities.description: Researcher and corresponding author of the study.

entities.description: Academic affiliation of the author in Australia.

entities.id: Biology Department, School of Education, Can Tho University, Vietnam
entities.description: Academic affiliation of the author in Vietnam.
The primary field of research concerning immune system functions and pathogen interactions.
The process of maturing bone marrow stem cells into macrophages.
The ability of macrophages to ingest and eliminate pathogens.
A bacterial infection used as a model to study host-pathogen interactions.
An alternative, cost-effective source of CSF-1 used for macrophage differentiation.
A required growth factor for the differentiation and survival of BMDM.
A technique used to detect and measure physical and chemical characteristics of a population of cells.
An imaging technique used to visualize specific proteins or pathogens in cells.
A procedure used to evaluate the interaction between a pathogen and host cells.
An antibiotic used to kill extracellular bacteria during infection assays.
The purity percentage of CD11b+ and F4/80+ macrophage population differentiated in LCM.
The standard temperature used for cell culture and bacterial incubation.
The ratio of bacteria to host cells used in the infection assay.
The time point used to measure the initial phagocytosis of LM by macrophages.
